data.title: Binance to Adjust Tick Size for USDⓈ-M Perpetual Futures Contracts
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the exchange is set to adjust the tick size for several USDⓈ-M Perpetual Futures Contracts to enhance market liquidity and improve the trading experience for users. The adjustments will take place on specific dates, beginning with LABUSDT, RAVEUSDT, and ESPORTSUSDT on 2026-04-21 at 06:30 (UTC). The tick size for these contracts will change from 0.00001 to 0.0001. 

Further adjustments are scheduled for 2026-04-22, affecting SNXUSDT, C98USDT, ONEUSDT, CELRUSDT, and XTZUSDT contracts. The tick size for SNXUSDT will change from 0.001 to 0.0001, while C98USDT will adjust from 0.0001 to 0.00001. ONEUSDT and CELRUSDT will see their tick sizes change from 0.00001 to 0.000001, and XTZUSDT will adjust from 0.001 to 0.0001. 

During the adjustment period, trading for the affected contracts will be temporarily suspended. For instance, the SNXUSDT contract will be suspended from 2026-04-22 06:30 (UTC) to 06:31 (UTC), while the C98USDT contract will be suspended from 06:45 (UTC) to 07:46 (UTC). Similar suspensions will occur for ONEUSDT, CELRUSDT, and XTZUSDT contracts. Users will not be able to place new orders or modify existing ones during these times. 

The status of these contracts will change from "TRADING" to "PENDING_TRADING" during the suspension. API users can query the latest status and tick size changes via the GET /fapi/v1/exchangeInfo endpoint. The tick size update will not affect existing orders, as they will still be matched with the original tick size. Users are advised to adjust their trading strategies accordingly to avoid any unnecessary impact.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts Peace Agreement Timeline Between Israel and Hezbollah
data.description:
Polymarket has introduced a new prediction market focused on the timeline for a permanent peace agreement between Israel and Hezbollah. According to Odaily, the probability of reaching such an agreement by April 26 is currently reported at 9%, while the likelihood of achieving it by May 31 stands at 29%.

A permanent peace agreement is defined as any accord that explicitly states the cessation or permanent halt of military hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah. Agreements that are temporary or do not include a final cessation of military hostilities, such as a temporary extension of a ceasefire announced on April 16, 2026, do not qualify.

The primary sources of information for this market are official statements from the Israeli government and Hezbollah, although other credible reports may also be considered.

data.title: RAVE Surges to New All-Time High, Exceeds $19
data.description: RAVE has experienced a significant price increase, surpassing $19.19 to reach a new all-time high. According to Odaily, the current price is reported at $18.77, marking a daily increase of over 31%.

data.title: ListaDAO Clarifies Unauthorized Contract Attack
data.description:
ListaDAO has issued a statement regarding a recent attack on a contract named 'ListaDAOLiquidStakingVault,' clarifying that the compromised contract was not officially deployed by them but was instead a counterfeit created by an unverified third party. According to ChainCatcher, the official contracts of ListaDAO remain unaffected by this incident.

The GoPlus security team conducted an in-depth analysis and found that the attack occurred on April 16, 2026. The root cause was identified as a business logic flaw in the third-party contract. This flaw allowed the triggering of the Dividend.setShares() function during token transfers, altering the share accounting within the contract and affecting the reward calculations in the claimReward() function. The attacker exploited this vulnerability to deplete the contract's assets.

GoPlus warns that the logic flaw exists in both segments of the contract code, posing a high risk of exploitation for any projects that fork or reuse this code. Developers are advised to promptly inspect and fix their code and implement continuous auditing mechanisms to ensure the security of smart contracts.

data.title: Significant BTC and ETH Options Expiry with Notable Put/Call Ratios
data.description: Approximately 22,000 Bitcoin options have expired, with a Put/Call ratio of 1.05 and a maximum pain point of $72,000, according to ChainCatcher. The nominal value of these options is around $1.65 billion. Additionally, about 100,000 Ethereum options have expired, featuring a Put/Call ratio of 0.95 and a maximum pain point of $2,250, with a nominal value of approximately $460 million.

data.title: India Permits 15 Banks to Import Gold and Silver from 2026 to 2029
data.description: India has granted permission to 15 banks to import gold and silver over a three-year period starting from April 1, 2026, to March 31, 2029. According to Jin10, this move is part of India's broader strategy to manage its precious metals market and ensure a steady supply of these commodities. The decision is expected to impact the domestic market dynamics and could influence global precious metals trading patterns. The selected banks will play a crucial role in facilitating the import process, potentially affecting pricing and availability in the Indian market.

data.title: UBS Report: Middle East Conflict Resolution Could Refocus Investors on Economic Fundamentals
data.description: UBS released a report on April 16, stating that a diplomatic resolution to the Middle East conflict could allow investors to refocus on healthy economic and earnings fundamentals. According to Jin10, the bank maintains that global and U.S. stock markets remain attractive despite the ongoing Middle East crisis. UBS anticipates a strong performance in the U.S. stock market for the first quarter earnings season, highlighting an optimistic outlook for corporate profits. Earnings per share are expected to grow by 17% this quarter, marking the fastest growth since the fourth quarter of 2021. However, UBS also foresees challenges on the path to a long-term ceasefire in the Middle East. Even if shipping through the Strait of Hormuz resumes, the global energy market will require time to normalize. Consequently, UBS recently revised its Brent crude oil price forecast for the end of June from $90 per barrel to $100 per barrel. Given the energy market outlook, UBS has become more cautious about stock markets most sensitive to high fuel costs, including the Eurozone and India, downgrading their ratings from 'attractive' to 'neutral.'

data.title: European Natural Gas Prices Expected to Drop Despite Supply Concerns
data.description: On April 17, Jin10 reported that despite ongoing concerns about disruptions in liquefied natural gas (LNG) supply from the Middle East, European natural gas prices are expected to decline by over 4% this week. According to analysts at ANZ Bank, the region faces competition with Asia for LNG cargoes, as increased imports are necessary to replenish depleted gas inventories. Forecasts of falling temperatures in Northwestern Europe are temporarily supporting fuel demand and slowing inventory accumulation. In early trading, the Dutch TTF benchmark futures contract fell by 1.6%, reaching 41.74 euros per megawatt-hour. Optimism surrounding U.S.-Iran negotiations and a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon has eased concerns over prolonged conflicts.

data.title: Tokyo Introduces Subsidy Program to Promote Yen Stablecoin Adoption
data.description:
Tokyo has launched a subsidy program aimed at promoting the use of yen stablecoins to build a digital economy and enhance the yen's status. According to Foresight News, the program, announced by the Tokyo Metropolitan Government's Bureau of Industrial and Labor Affairs on April 15, supports projects utilizing yen stablecoins issued within Japan for transactions and remittances. The maximum subsidy per project is set at 40 million yen, covering up to two-thirds of related expenses.

The subsidy covers costs such as external platform usage fees, including issuance platforms, wallets, and blockchain node fees, as well as expert legal and audit consultation fees and system development costs. Eligible applicants must have a registered headquarters or branch in Tokyo, and the verification or implementation of the related projects should be completed by the end of the fiscal year in which the subsidy is granted.

data.title: OneBullEx to Launch AI Strategy Tool Testing in 2026
data.description:
OneBullEx has announced that it will begin targeted testing of its AI strategy tool, OneAlpha, on April 20, 2026. According to ChainCatcher, the testing aims to enhance product experience and gather user feedback.

OneAlpha is designed for futures trading scenarios, allowing users to generate trading strategies using natural language. The platform facilitates the entire process from strategy creation to validation, optimization, and deployment.

The internal testing will run from April 20 to May 3, 2026, lasting two weeks. Participation will be through selective invitations, with registration closing on April 26. Interested users can contact OneBullEx through official channels for more information on how to participate.

data.title: Pinggao Shares Warns of Potential Stock Volatility
data.description:
Pinggao Shares issued a notice on April 17 regarding unusual stock activity, noting that the company's stock has seen significant gains, surpassing indices such as the Sci-Tech Innovation Composite Index and the Sci-Tech 50 Index. According to Jin10, this rapid increase may pose a risk of a subsequent decline. The company stated that its fundamentals have not undergone significant changes, and there is no undisclosed major information.

Jiangyuan Technology, an associate company of Pinggao, is not included in Pinggao's consolidated financial statements and does not significantly impact its operations. Recently, Jiangyuan Technology's D10AI accelerator card was selected in the GPU model for the 2026 portable computing service technical specification by China Mobile (Suzhou) Software Technology Co., Ltd. However, the current procurement amount is small and does not significantly affect Jiangyuan Technology's operations.

Jiangyuan Technology is still in its early investment stage and may face various risks in its future development, including macroeconomic factors, industry policies, and its own business cycle. These could lead to the failure to achieve expected benefits or even result in investment losses and impairment.

data.title: Expert Calls for DeFi Shift from High Yields to Risk Management
data.description: Juan Manuel Pellicer, vice president of research at Sentora, emphasized the need for decentralized finance (DeFi) platforms to prioritize risk management over marketing high yields. According to NS3.AI, Pellicer noted that many DeFi vaults concentrate on annual percentage yields (APY) without transparently offering users specific risk metrics. This approach, he argues, could lead to increased vulnerability for investors who may not fully understand the risks involved in their investments.

data.title: Zipangcoin's Transition to OP Mainnet Marks First Public Blockchain Issuance
data.description: Mitsui & Co. Digital Commodities has successfully transitioned Zipangcoin (ZPG) to the OP Mainnet, marking the tokenized metals asset's inaugural issuance on a public blockchain. According to NS3.AI, this move signifies a significant step in the asset's evolution from its previous operation under Japan's regulatory framework on bitFlyer's private Miyabi blockchain since 2022. In addition, Japanese cryptocurrency exchange GMO Coin is set to list ZPG on April 20, further expanding its accessibility to investors.

data.title: Mitsubishi UFJ: Gold futures are poised for a weekly gain.
data.description: Gold futures are poised for a weekly gain as optimism surrounding potential talks between the US and Iran and a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon is weighing on oil prices and boosting overall market sentiment. Mitsubishi UFJ stated, "Lower oil prices have eased inflation concerns." However, while gold has recovered some of its earlier losses, it remains about 9% lower than at the start of the conflict, and continued risks to energy supplies and the presence of damaged infrastructure could keep inflation uncertainty high. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin Miners Shift Focus to AI Amid Declining Mining Revenue
data.description:
Bitcoin miners are increasingly pivoting towards artificial intelligence (AI) ventures, according to Charles Edwards, founder of Capriole. According to Foresight News, Edwards noted that all major publicly listed Bitcoin mining companies have announced significant shifts to AI operations. He predicts that within two to three years, the proportion of revenue from Bitcoin mining will drop from the current 90% to just 30%. Edwards expressed concern over this trend, especially with the looming threat of quantum computing, as the security foundation of the Bitcoin network, based on computational power, is rapidly diminishing. Many miners are reportedly not planning to upgrade or purchase new Bitcoin mining equipment.

In response, Adam Back, CEO of Blockstream, suggested that this shift could be beneficial for miners. He explained that if the overall network's computational power decreases, mining profit margins could actually increase. This situation presents an arbitrage opportunity, eventually leading to an equilibrium where mining profit margins align with those of AI workloads. Higher profit margins could create a positive feedback loop, where miners sell less Bitcoin to cover electricity costs, and as Bitcoin prices rise, profit margins would further improve.

data.title: Hong Kong Halts Basketball Betting Project Amid Concerns Over Illegal Gambling
data.description:
The Hong Kong government has decided to halt the advancement of a basketball betting project, according to a report by Hong Kong Radio on April 17. According to BlockBeats, Secretary for Home and Youth Affairs, Mak Mei-juan, stated that the rapid development of the 'prediction market' in recent years has raised concerns. Monthly trading volumes have surged from less than $100 million two years ago to over $13 billion last year, with nearly 40% of transactions related to sports. Authorities worry that introducing a new betting project now could increase public interest and participation in illegal gambling within the 'prediction market.'

Mak emphasized that existing regulated betting projects have established mature monitoring and management systems, which help control risks. However, the uncertainty surrounding the 'prediction market' necessitates further research to understand its impact and prevalence in Hong Kong. The government maintains close contact with the Hong Kong Jockey Club, which has a stringent monitoring system for illegal gambling and employs experts to combat such activities. The government will collaborate with the Jockey Club to develop measures specifically targeting illegal gambling.

Mak reiterated the government's longstanding policy of discouraging gambling. She noted that betting on sports events within the 'prediction market' constitutes illegal gambling. The authorities plan to enhance public education and awareness campaigns to highlight the dangers of gambling and discourage participation in illegal activities.

data.title: Middle East Sees Increased Dealmaking Amid Regional Disruptions
data.description: Abu Dhabi's Axight is contributing to a surge in dealmaking activities across the Middle East, despite ongoing disruptions caused by the Iran war. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the resilience of the region's business environment in the face of geopolitical tensions. The Middle East has witnessed a series of significant deals, underscoring the area's strategic importance and economic potential. Companies are navigating challenges posed by regional conflicts, yet continue to pursue growth and expansion opportunities. This trend reflects a broader determination to maintain economic momentum and capitalize on emerging markets within the region.

data.title: Binance Unveils Capital Connect Upgrade and Wallet Enhancements
data.description:
Binance announced on X the latest updates to its platform, focusing on enhancing user experience and connectivity. The key highlight is the upgrade to Capital Connect, which now features a strategy marketplace. This new addition aims to facilitate direct connections between professional trading teams and institutional investors, making collaboration more accessible. This development is expected to streamline interactions and foster a more integrated trading environment.

In addition to the Capital Connect upgrade, Binance has introduced significant enhancements to its wallet interface. The updated wallet now includes sliding cards on the market page, providing users with a more intuitive and efficient way to navigate through market data. Furthermore, a customizable quick access feature has been added, allowing users to tailor their experience according to their preferences. These improvements are designed to make operations smoother and more user-friendly, reflecting Binance's commitment to continuous innovation and user satisfaction.

data.title: Japan's Largest Union Reports Wage Increase Over 5%
data.description:
Japan's largest labor union, the Japanese Trade Union Confederation (Rengo), announced that the average wage increase for employees of its member companies has exceeded 5% in this year's wage negotiations. According to Jin10, this trend persists even when considering the results from smaller enterprises. This development is expected to facilitate the Bank of Japan's short-term interest rate hike goals.

The latest statistics released on Friday indicate that unions affiliated with Rengo have achieved an average wage growth of 5.08%. Although this figure is slightly lower than the initially reported 5.26%, it remains above the organization's 5% target for the third consecutive year. The recent data shows that 2,156 companies with 300 or fewer employees have reached wage increase agreements, a significant rise from the 552 companies reported in March's initial report. Smaller companies typically report wage agreements later than larger firms, which has contributed to a decrease in the overall average in recent years.

data.title: UBS Report: Fed Expected to Cut Rates by 50 Basis Points Later This Year
data.description: UBS released a report on April 17, indicating that the Federal Reserve remains on a path toward further easing. According to Jin10, Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell recently downplayed the need to tighten monetary policy due to rising energy prices, noting that policymakers typically 'ignore' supply shocks like oil price surges, especially when inflation expectations remain well-controlled. Although the Fed is still seeking further evidence of a sustained decline in core inflation before implementing additional easing measures, UBS still expects a 50 basis point rate cut later this year. Given that U.S. Treasury yields are significantly higher than pre-conflict levels, UBS believes there is ample room for them to decrease. The year-end targets for the 2-year and 10-year Treasury yields are set at 3.25% and 3.75%, respectively.
